ranked_movies.title: Parasite
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a masterpiece and a key reason to choose Bong Joon-ho's filmography. Won the Palme d'Or and Best Picture Oscar, making it one of the most acclaimed films discussed.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: Described as one of the best films ever seen by multiple commenters. A cornerstone of Park Chan-wook's filmography and a defining film of the revenge thriller genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Handmaiden
ranked_movies.reason: Highly praised and considered by some to be superior to 'Parasite'. Noted for its aesthetics, storytelling, and being a prime example of Park Chan-wook's versatility and skill.

ranked_movies.title: Memories of Murder
ranked_movies.reason: Called the best film from either director's catalog by one user. A highly influential crime thriller from Bong Joon-ho that is consistently mentioned as a top-tier work.

ranked_movies.title: Decision to Leave
ranked_movies.reason: Ranked alongside 'The Handmaiden' as a superior film to Bong's works by some. Highlighted as a recent example of Park Chan-wook's consistent excellence in romance and thrillers.

ranked_movies.title: Mother
ranked_movies.reason: Grouped with 'Parasite' and 'Memories of Murder' as one of Bong Joon-ho's top-notch films, indicating its high standing among his work.

ranked_movies.title: The Chaser
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in the context of 'The Yellow Sea' as an arguably better (though harder to watch) film by the same director, Na Hong-jin. A benchmark for Korean thrillers.

ranked_movies.title: Save the Green Planet!
ranked_movies.reason: Heavily recommended as an underappreciated gem. Its upcoming English-language remake, 'Bugonia', directed by Yorgos Lanthimos, was also a major point of discussion in the box office forecast.

ranked_movies.title: I Saw the Devil
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended alongside 'The Good, The Bad and The Weird' as an awesome movie from director Kim Jee-woon, another highly regarded Korean filmmaker.

ranked_movies.title: The Good, The Bad and The Weird
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as an awesome movie from director Kim Jee-woon, often mentioned in the same breath as other classic Korean genre films.

ranked_movies.title: A Dirty Carnival
ranked_movies.reason: Specifically recommended as an underappreciated Korean movie from 2006.

ranked_movies.title: Peppermint Candy
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a powerful, older Korean film (1999) that has a lasting impact on viewers, particularly its ending.

ranked_movies.title: Sea Fog
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an underappreciated Korean movie from 2014.

ranked_movies.title: Welcome to Dongmakgol
ranked_movies.reason: Asked about as a potential underappreciated film, indicating its status as a notable but perhaps lesser-discussed title.

ranked_movies.title: Asura: The City of Madness
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an underappreciated Korean movie from 2016.

ranked_movies.title: Train to Busan
ranked_movies.reason: Implicitly referenced in a 'Star Trek' post title ('Shuttle to Kenfori'), confirming its iconic status as the definitive South Korean zombie movie.

ranked_movies.title: Decibel
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a Korean film the user liked and saw in the theater in 2022.

ranked_movies.title: The Host
ranked_movies.reason: While not directly named, Bong Joon-ho's filmography was discussed broadly, and 'The Host' is a major part of it, often grouped with his other well-known works.

ranked_movies.title: Sympathy for Lady Vengeance
ranked_movies.reason: As part of Park Chan-wook's celebrated Vengeance Trilogy, which includes 'Oldboy', it is a significant part of his highly recommended filmography.

ranked_movies.title: Joint Security Area
ranked_movies.reason: Another early, acclaimed work from Park Chan-wook that contributes to his reputation for consistency and quality, though not explicitly named in these threads.
